pyqgis_plugin.name: Raster to GPKG converter
pyqgis_plugin.description: This plugin converts any raster to a GeoPackage. It builds also the piramids.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin is an internal PCA QGIS plugin that converts any raster to a new GeoPackage raster layer with calculated piramids.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, raster, piramids, gpkg, conversion. orthophoto

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Geomax Survey Processing
pyqgis_plugin.description: PCA Geomax Survey Processing is an internal QGIS plugin designed to manage and process PCA survey data efficiently.
pyqgis_plugin.about:
PCA Geomax Survey Processing is an internal QGIS plugin created specifically for managing and processing PCA survey data.
This plugin is a core component of the new DRS/GIS integrated data management system, utilizing the latest Geomax codelist system and raw survey data exported in shapefile format.
The current version of the plugin supports Geomax Codelist version draft 4.2. A dedicated help section is available in the toolbar, offering more information and detailed usage instructions.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, Geomax, survey, processing, DRS

pyqgis_plugin.name: Evaluation Trenches Generator
pyqgis_plugin.description: This plugin provide a set of tools to generates trenches for archaeological evaluations.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin provide a set of tools to generates trenches for archaeological evaluations and to generate the necessary files to configurate the GNSS setout.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, python, archaeology, evaluation, trench, setout

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Post-excavation
pyqgis_plugin.description: Manage archaeological post-excavation data, update Interventions and DRS records, and prepare standardized exports directly within QGIS.
pyqgis_plugin.about: about=PCA Post-Excavation is a QGIS plugin designed to support archaeological post-excavation workflows through a suite of productivity and data management tools. It helps maintain consistent Groups, Entities, Periods, Sub-Periods and Phases, update related datasets, and prepare information for reporting and analysis. Developed by the PCA Geospatial Data Team for everyday archaeological GIS use.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, python, archaeology, post-excavation, phasing, grouping, periods

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA DRS Context Checks
pyqgis_plugin.description: This is an internal QGIS Plugin that provides a quick access to a array of queries built for check the PCA DRS Spreadsheet during the post-excavation phase.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin provides a quick access to an array of queries built for check the PCA DRS Context spreadsheet during the post-excavation phase. It is a component of the new DRS/GIS integrated data management system. It provides a dockable panel that allows to run some checks, as missing context sheets, duplicated context numbers and recorded features not matching the site plan.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, DRS, spreadsheet, postexcavation, checks, duplicates

pyqgis_plugin.name: Search Value
pyqgis_plugin.description: A dock widget for searching values on the layers attribute table and for zooming on the relative geometries on the map.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin opens a dock widget that allows searching a value on a layer attribute table. When a value is found, the tool zooms to the relative geometry on the map.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, search, zoom, attribute table

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Report Description Generator
pyqgis_plugin.description: Generate archaeological report descriptions directly from DRS context records. Supports cuts, groups and individual contexts with dedicated templates for fills, masonry, skeletons, cremations, layers and structures.
pyqgis_plugin.about:
PCA Report Description Generator creates draft archaeological report text directly from DRS context records within QGIS.
The plugin supports generation of Cut, Group and Context descriptions using dedicated templates tailored to individual archaeological record types, including fills, layers, masonry, skeletons, cremations and structures.
Descriptions are generated only from information recorded on the context sheet, helping maintain consistency between field records and reporting while allowing archaeologists to review and refine the final text before publication.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, PCA, report, groups, attribute table, string, description, DRS

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA DRS Context Sheet Printer
pyqgis_plugin.description: Generate clean printable PDF recording sheets directly from PCA DRS databases using native QGIS layouts.
pyqgis_plugin.about: PCA DRS Context Sheet Printer is an internal QGIS plugin developed for the PCA DRS/GIS integrated workflow. The plugin generates compact one-page printable PDF recording sheets directly from PCA DRS databases, including context, trench, cremation, and skeleton records, using fully native QGIS print layouts.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, PCA, DRS, context, trench, printing

pyqgis_plugin.name: Check Duplicated Features
pyqgis_plugin.description: This plugin checks for duplicated features on a layer.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin checks for duplicated features on a layer. Optionally, you can increment the check's factors (e.g. geometry and attribute value). After the check is possible to decide if immediately delete the features result of the check.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, PCA, geometry, duplicate

pyqgis_plugin.name: Geopackage to SHP Exporter
pyqgis_plugin.description: This plugin exports each layer contained in a Geopackage to a separate shapefile.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin exports each layer contained in a Geopackage to a separate shapefile. Empty and no-geometric layers will be discarded.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, PCA, gpkg, export

pyqgis_plugin.name: Add Save All Edits Shortcut
pyqgis_plugin.description: This plugin add a shortcut for quick access to the option Save Edits for All Layers.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin add a shortcut for quick access to the option Save Edits for All Layers.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, shortcut, saveall, PCA

pyqgis_plugin.name: Coordinate Plotter
pyqgis_plugin.description: This plugin plots coordinates from user-inputted values.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin plots coordinates from user-inputted values.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, coordinates, PCA, Pre-construct Archaeology, point

pyqgis_plugin.name: Mergin Projects Permits Adder
pyqgis_plugin.description: This is a QGIS plugin for easy integration and management of Mergin projects permits.
pyqgis_plugin.about: Mergin Projects Permit Adder is a QGIS plugin that streamlines the addition and management of Mergin projects in QGIS settings (QGIS.ini). It prevents duplication, allows folder path updates, and offers an intuitive user interface for seamless project integration.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, Mergin, PCA, ini, merginmaps, synchronisation

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Finds Distribution Generator
pyqgis_plugin.description: Creates archaeological finds distribution layers from finds catalogues with optional grouped summaries for contexts, cuts, or other recording units.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin helps create archaeological finds distribution layers directly from finds catalogues. It can also generate optional grouped summaries for contexts, cuts, or other recording units with combined finds quantities and weights. The tool is designed to simplify and speed up the preparation of finds distribution outputs while keeping workflows consistent and easy to manage.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, pca, finds, distribution, aggregate, pottery, lithic, catalogue, csv

pyqgis_plugin.name: UK National Grid Map Reference Finder
pyqgis_plugin.description: Zooms the map canvas to a given UK National Grid Reference and briefly highlights the location.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin allows you to quickly navigate to any location using a UK National Grid Map Reference (e.g. TQ 17304 91389). After entering the grid reference, the map canvas automatically zooms to the corresponding position and displays a temporary blinking marker to clearly highlight the location for a few seconds. This is especially useful when working from written coordinates in reports, emails, or field notes and needing to locate them rapidly in QGIS.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, pca, UK, grid, reference, finder, coordinates

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Geometry Quality Control
pyqgis_plugin.description: Checks all vector layers in the project for geometry issues and highlights problems to help ensure clean, reliable GIS data.
pyqgis_plugin.about:
This tool scans all vector layers in your QGIS project and identifies geometries that may cause problems during analysis or map production. It automatically detects null, empty, and invalid geometries, summarises issues in an easy-to-read panel, and provides quick actions to review, select, or remove problematic features.
Layers can be excluded from checks using a simple layer variable (skip_geometry_check = true), and full reports can be copied or exported for documentation.
Designed to support clean, consistent datasets across large or complex GIS projects.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, geometry, validation, quality control, null geometries, PCA, Pre-Construct Archaeology

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Data Source Explorer
pyqgis_plugin.description: View and organise all data sources in a QGIS project. Groups layers by file location, highlights missing and external data, and separates database and online sources.
pyqgis_plugin.about:
This tool provides a structured overview of all data sources used in a QGIS project. File-based layers are grouped by folder and file, making it easier to understand where data is stored and how it is organised. Sources are classified to distinguish between data stored within the project folder, external files, and missing paths.
Single-layer formats such as shapefiles, CSV and raster files are displayed in a compact form, while multi-layer formats like GeoPackage and DXF are expanded to show their internal structure. Database connections and online services are listed separately to keep non-file sources clearly identified.
The panel updates automatically when the project changes and allows quick access to file locations. A text report can be generated and saved, including project name, project path, and the date and time of export.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: python, data sources, explorer, qgis, layers, files, geopackage, database

pyqgis_plugin.name: Replace Clipped Geometry
pyqgis_plugin.description: Professional geometry replacement tool for QGIS with advanced snapping-aware digitising, optional polygon clipping, live preview and configurable replacement workflow while preserving all feature attributes.
pyqgis_plugin.about:
The Replace Clipped Geometry plugin provides a professional geometry replacement workflow for QGIS while preserving all existing feature attributes and metadata. The tool supports point, line and polygon layers and works with any editable vector layer format supported by QGIS.
The plugin includes a fully autonomous digitising workflow based on QgsMapToolAdvancedDigitizing, providing native QGIS snapping and CAD integration without relying on the standard QGIS Add Feature tool or temporary feature creation.
Optional clipping allows the replacement geometry to be automatically constrained to a selected polygon layer, supporting both selected-feature and automatic intersecting-feature workflows.
Designed for professional GIS editing, CAD-style workflows and archaeological / geospatial production environments.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, attribute, digitizing, geometry, point, polygon, polyline, replace, survey, clip

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Fieldwalk Grid Generator
pyqgis_plugin.description: Generate PCA fieldwalking hectare and custom collection grids from a site boundary layer.
pyqgis_plugin.about:
PCA Fieldwalk Grid Generator is a QGIS plugin developed for the rapid creation of standardised archaeological fieldwalking grids based on a site boundary.
The tool generates a British National Grid aligned 100 m hectare grid together with an optional detailed collection grid using user-defined rectangular cell sizes. Generated grids are automatically labelled using a consistent PCA fieldwalking reference system and saved directly into the project GeoPackage with embedded PCA styles.
The plugin is designed for geomaticians and archaeological GIS workflows where accurate, repeatable and spatially consistent survey grids are required for finds collection, fieldwalking survey, and spatial recording.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: PCA, grid, fieldwalking, survey, grid generator, field survey

pyqgis_plugin.name: PCA Export Phased Features
pyqgis_plugin.description: Exports Features_for_PostEx polygons into grouped ESRI Shapefiles based on post-excavation phasing fields.
pyqgis_plugin.about: This plugin exports Features_for_PostEx polygons into separate phased ESRI Shapefiles for post-excavation workflows. Includes export validation, geometry safety checks, export preview, QA logging, and QGIS 3/QGIS 4 compatibility support.
pyqgis_plugin.tags: postexcavation, archaeology, export, phasing, features, pca, geospatial
